title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner from UNC as the 20th pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th prospect by MLB.com, had a .356 average, 85 runs, and 50 RBIs in 68 games.
Defensively, Schaffner committed just three errors with a .987 fielding percentage, indicating strong shortstop performance.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The USS Constitution led the Sail Boston 2026 Grand Parade of Sail, a rare gathering of historic ships.
Past events like the 2016 Tall Ships predicted the return of such celebrations, emphasizing their generational significance.
The parade featured a mix of international vessels, including Nova Scotia's Bluenose Two and Massachusetts' Schooner Adventure.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner by The Athletic for his poor performance with a 10.35 ERA.
Bello allowed 41 earned runs over 35.2 innings in eight starts for the Boston Red Sox before being sent to Triple-A.
Despite a strong relief performance with a 0.71 ERA, Bello remained in Triple-A as Boston recalled Eduardo Rivera instead.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, two games behind the final AL wild-card spot.
Boston faces a decision: enhance their roster for a playoff run or trade players for future assets.
Past trade deadlines have seen indecision, impacting the team's immediate competitiveness and future development.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden on July 10, 2026, as part of her Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour.
The concert included a mix of crowd favorites and new songs from her Grammy-winning 2025 album.
Visual spectacles included fiery stage effects and a giant inflatable she-wolf, enhancing the two-hour show.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Healey-Driscoll administration announced $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects will convert historic buildings and mills into residential units in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
The tax relief package increases funding for the Housing Development Incentive Program, supporting economic revitalization in mid-sized communities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: FBI arrested Joseph Sawyer, 52, in Albany, NY, for alleged armed bank robberies in NH and NJ last month.
Sawyer allegedly stole $6,000 from a Nashua bank and $12,000 from a Boonton bank, both armed heists.
FBI's Operation Summer Heat 2.0 involved multiple agencies from Boston, Albany, and New Jersey to locate Sawyer.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near Route 2 in Shelburne, NH, next to his motorcycle on July 10.
Berlin Police requested assistance from NH State Police on July 10 after Grondin went missing on July 4.
Preliminary investigation shows Grondin crossed into the eastbound lane, struck a post; no other vehicles were involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Kahan made history with four sold-out shows at Fenway Park, attracting over 150,000 fans.
Kahan is recognized for his music and advocacy for mental health issues through the Busyhead Project.

title: ‘Let’s just call it a tour’: Judy Collins says her farewell tour isn’t a farewell
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Judy Collins, 87, clarifies her 'Sweet Judy Blue Eyes' tour as not being a farewell, despite being billed as such.
Collins' tour includes performances at Cape Cod, Cohasset, Bar Harbor, and Tanglewood, featuring artists like Rosanne Cash and Richard Thompson.
The tour will be filmed for a documentary on Collins' life and legacy, running through March 2027, two months before her 88th birthday.
